summary: Windows documents the machine-level CapabilityAccessManager location consent-store value as an exact runtime control surface. The app writes that same HKLM path with Value = Deny.

setting.name: Location Consent Store (System)
setting.casual_explanation: This changes the system-level location permission state to deny access.
setting.why_people_change_it: People use this when they want a stronger device-wide deny stance for location access.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: It can be stricter than user-level choices, but it is a runtime consent surface rather than a Group Policy-backed location control.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's location troubleshooting guidance documents the exact machine-level CapabilityAccessManager consent-store path and says Value can be set to Allow or Deny under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore\location. The app writes that exact runtime surface, not a policy-backed LocationAndSensors control.

allowed_values.label: Normal system consent baseline
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses the normal location consent behavior for the device.

allowed_values.label: System-level allow
allowed_values.meaning: The system-level runtime consent value explicitly allows device location access.

allowed_values.label: System-level deny
allowed_values.meaning: The current app writes a device-level deny state for location access.

app_current_implementation.notes: The provider writes Value = Deny under the machine-level location consent-store path, which matches the Microsoft-documented runtime control surface.

states.rationale: Leave the runtime consent-store value absent and let Windows manage the normal device-level consent behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: People explicitly reproducing the current app behavior

states.rationale: Leave the runtime consent-store value absent.

recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a system-level deny state right now.
tradeoffs: Does not explicitly force device-level denial.

recommended_profiles.label: Current system deny

states.rationale: Track the system-level deny state the app writes.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting or testing the current device-level behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a clean validated location policy.
tradeoffs: Device location-aware features can stop working correctly.

evidence.title: Microsoft location consent runtime registry guidance
evidence.summary: Microsoft troubleshooting guidance documents the exact HKLM CapabilityAccessManager location consent-store path and states that Value can be set to Allow or Deny.

evidence.title: Current privacy provider system location-consent write

decision.why: Microsoft troubleshooting guidance documents the exact machine-level CapabilityAccessManager location consent-store path and says the Value can be set to Allow or Deny. The app writes that same runtime control surface with Value = Deny.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Go to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore\location. Use the following registry value to set the location setting. Value: Allow or Deny.
validation_proof.notes: This is an official runtime consent-store surface from Microsoft troubleshooting guidance, not a Group Policy-backed LocationAndSensors setting.
